Patch Set 3:

James,

You're welcome to write that patch instead if you'd like, but this one is simpler.  Why create that churn?

Also, some people in the Lustre community like strlcpy enough to use it instead, even though they had to add it.  Frankly, the argument over string handlers verges on the religious, and I'm happy to let everyone have what they want.  This lets us do that. ;)

Patch Set 3:

Its not strlcpy I hate. Its libcfs.a I dislike. I really like to see libcfs.a just go away. This can happen by breaking it up and putting what is LNet related into liblnetconfig and lustre specific code can go into liblustreapi. I already have been moving in that direction. See patch  https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/28752. I see libcfs.a strlcpy as extra implementation cruft that doesn't really belong in liblnetconfig or liblustreapi. If people really want it then lets make libbsd a requirement instead.

Patch Set 3:

BTW with patch LU-9897 liblusterapi.so is the only user of strlcpy and strlcat. In theory this code could  be moved from libcfs.a and placed into liblustreapi.so.

I don't think so (about Slurm), but I have no idea.  Sorry.

WRT the code elimination stuff...  I sort of have no opinion there.  If you want to take on getting that working, great.  I think this could be merged separately from all that.

If liblustreapi is the only user of strlcpy() then it makes sense to move the compat code over there. Then a separate patch to remove it could be landed, but at least we are shrinking libcfs.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

With the landing of LU-9897 the only user outside of liblustreapi.so is param.c in the libcfs/util directory. That single piece of code could easily be replaced with snprintf and the strlcpy and strlcat functions can be merged into liblusterapi.so.

Also as I pointed out external strl{cpy,cat} are never going to be linked into liblustreapi since their are to many libraries in the wild that provide their own version of strl{cpy,cat}. The chance of collision is way to high.

Patch Set 5:

Just want to make something clear:

I don't have a lot of time to spend on this one, so if other people feel strongly, I'm going to have to push them to sort out.  Cray's itch is scratched by this patch as is, if others want to do a deeper rewrite/fix, I have to leave that to those who want that.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

Still can collide with other libraries. Also this breaks GSS keyring.

Patch Set 6:

I'll buy "breaks GSS", though I'd like to see the compiler errors so I can try to fix it...  But I don't buy can still collide.  How so?

Patch Set 6:

The new patch with the new name has exactly the same collision properties as this one.  The symbol strlcpy *DOES NOT EXIST IN THE COMPILED BINARIES* with this patch.

Have I missed something?

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Please see my comment in libcfs/util/string.h. BTW libmungle fixed their strl* issues exactly as I purposed. See

https://github.com/dun/munge/issues/49

Patch Set 6:

Since you most likely want to backport this I think I have an idea to get around the strlcpy header collisions. Lustre uses #ifdef LUSTRE_UTILS to build code for the case of lustre tools but not userland applications. I think that can provide what you are looking for and avoid any problems.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Abandoned

Superseded by other patch

parents.subject: LU-10041 osd: osd-zfs to choose dnode size

commit.subject: LU-8854 llapi: do not define strlcpy & strlcat
commit.message:
LU-8854 llapi: do not define strlcpy & strlcat

LustreAPI should not define/export stlrcpy and strlcat,
because they may be defined by other libraries which
people want to link against.

Instead, use the preprocessor to let us keep calling
strl{cpy,cat}, while really defining ll_strl{cpy,cat}.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Change-Id: I8270b97af87d69711d65a55a1357f8849019a86b
